text: PHA may not demolish or dispose of public housing without HUD approval and must continue ACC obligations while application is pending.
Section 18 application must include plan alignment, relocation plan, consultation evidence, board resolution, environmental review, and civil-rights certification.
PHA must consult affected residents, resident organizations, and RAB, and failures can support disapproval.
PHA must notify each family 90 days before displacement except imminent threat to health and safety.
Demolition or disposition completion may not occur until all residents in the building are relocated.
Comparable housing must meet HQS and be in an area generally not less desirable than prior housing.
Comparable relocation for displaced persons with disabilities should include similar accommodations and reasonable accommodation protections.
PHA must provide actual and reasonable relocation expenses and necessary counseling for displaced residents.
In disposition, resident entities have 30 days to express interest and 60 days to secure firm financing commitment.
Net disposition proceeds must be used first for debt retirement unless waived, then for low-income housing or resident-benefit uses.
HUD must disapprove where certifications conflict with data or where required consultation with residents/local officials is missing.
URA is generally inapplicable to Section 18 actions, but CDBG/HOME/UDAG funding can trigger section 104(d) anti-displacement obligations.
